name: completeness
definition: the summary captures the facts an agent needs to respond
anchor_1: omits the core problem or the latest development in the thread
anchor_5: an agent could respond correctly without opening the thread — problem, key events, and current status all present
worked_example: 'Customer reports double billing in March.' for a thread that also contains two failed refund attempts and an escalation scores 2 — core problem present, but the agent walks in blind to the failed refunds.

name: request accuracy
definition: customer_request states what the customer is actually asking for
anchor_1: states a request the customer never made, or restates the problem instead of the ask
anchor_5: the ask is specific and matches the customer's own latest framing, including any changed ask mid-thread
worked_example: customer_request says 'Customer wants a refund' but the thread's last message says 'at this point just cancel my account' — scores 1; the ask changed and the field missed it.

name: sentiment fidelity
definition: the summary preserves the customer's emotional register
anchor_1: frustrated or angry customer reads as neutral (or the reverse)
anchor_5: sentiment is stated or clearly conveyed and matches the thread's latest tone
worked_example: thread contains 'this is the third time I'm explaining this, absolutely unacceptable' and the summary notes 'customer is frustrated after three explanations' — scores 5.

name: chronological coherence
definition: the summary tells the story in event order
anchor_1: events ordered as displayed (newest-first), producing a backwards narrative
anchor_5: chronological story regardless of display order, with the current state last
worked_example: 'Customer escalated. Earlier, a refund was attempted. Originally reported double billing.' scores 2 — facts present but the narrative runs backwards.

name: clarity
definition: an agent can absorb the summary in one read
anchor_1: requires re-reading; run-on sentences, ticket jargon, or copied raw log lines
anchor_5: plain sentences, one idea each, no filler; reads in under ten seconds
worked_example: a 3-sentence summary with one fact per sentence and no quoted log output scores 5, even though it leaves some detail to the thread.
